steps.title: Identify the defining trait Louis displays
steps.content: The passage opens with a metaphor—“tended the petition like a garden”—that signals devoted, careful attention. Every detail that follows reinforces this: keeping it with him at all times, his sharpened vigilance for unsigned members, actively cornering people in any location (gas pump, store, café, road, clinic), and securing signatures regardless of circumstance (childbirth, laughter, argument, school pickup). This is a portrait of relentless, unwavering commitment.

steps.title: Connect the behavioral details to the answer choices
steps.content:
Louis's actions—tending the petition as though cultivating a garden, keeping it constantly with him, alerting to any unsigned member, pursuing signatures in every setting and circumstance—reveal a person who is deeply, consistently committed to this task. Choices A and B discuss member dislike and changing attitudes that the passage never shows; choice C misplaces the focus on community enthusiasm rather than Louis's driving commitment.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
